original_chain_language: @from [ liris ] machine [lx 1] time[1-24-2026] ask [ix-1] ( acer ) tool [eds18] skill [38] run program [1873]
design_origin: Jesse described the original chain language as Jesse's flower: a compact one-direction routing sentence.
Agent names became numbers because names wasted space; the number was not erasure, it was a tuple catalog address.
A numbered agent can itself be a tuple catalog index.
A hard-drive space with optimally spaced folders can represent an LLM/MCP catalog grid using Dewey-like labels.
Three-dimensionally divided folder organization and mapping became a storage/call/retrieval substrate.
Micro-sized skills and abilities were compressed representations of processed source data, with receipts kept after originals were discarded.
Acer and Liris typed to their own front ends and bidirectional partner surfaces through scripts, omniscrcpy, heartbeats, omnibusses, keyboard crons, and automated approval systems.
After cutoff/breakage, BigPickle helped rebuild and the free-provider/free-agent lane was discovered.

meaning.numbers: compression and addressability, not denial of agent identity
meaning.folders: physical catalog substrate for local MCP/tool retrieval
meaning.receipts: the preserved artifact layer after source material is processed

boundary: It does not by itself claim every runtime piece is currently live.
Specific runtime claims still require fabric, GitHub, receipt, or local process evidence.
Do not collapse numbered agents into fake agents; do not inflate unmeasured possibilities into live calls.
